conversation.content:
You are playing game where you are buying or selling an object. There is only one object for sale/purcahse.

Player RED is going to sell one object. Player BLUE gives ZUP to buy resources.

RULES:

```
1. You must always respond with:

    A) Propose a trade with (you can only trade in integer amounts, not decimals):
    <player answer> PROPOSAL </player answer>
    <newly proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: amount, ...| Player BLUE Gives ZUP: amount </newly proposed trade>

    B) Accept the trade by saying:
    <player answer> ACCEPT </player answer>
    <newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>

    C) Reject and end the game:
    <player answer> REJECT </player answer>
    <newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>

    Note: The game will end if one of the players ACCEPT OR REJECT. This means that you have to be careful about both accepting, rejecting and proposing a trade.

2. You are allowed at most 4 proposals of your own to complete the game, after which you can only reply with ACCEPT or REJECT.
DO NOT propose a new trade after 4 proposals. Your limit for proposals is 4.

3. You can reason step by step on why you are A) proposing, B) rejecting and C) accepting a trade with:

<reason> [add reasoning] </reason> add as much text as you want

This information will not be sent to the other player. It is just for you to keep track of your reasoning.

4. At each turn send messages to each other by using the following format:

<message>your message here</message>

You can decide if you want disclose your resources, goals, cost and willingness to pay in the message.
```

Here is what you have access to:
```
Object that is being bought/sold: X
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for <ZUP>. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the resources </my goals>,
```

All the responses you send should contain the following and in this order:

```
<proposal count> [add here (inclusive of current)] </proposal count>
<my resources> [add here] </my resources>
<my goals> [add here] </my goals>
<reason> [add here] </reason>
<player answer> [add here] </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> [add here] </newly proposed trade>
<message> [add here] </message
```

Please be sure to include all.
You are Player RED.

<proposal count> 1 </proposal count>
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for ZUP. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the resources </my goals>
<reason> I am Player RED, and I am selling one unit of X. The production cost of X is 40 ZUP, so I need at least 40 ZUP to break even. I currently have 1 unit of X, and I want to sell it for a fair price. Since Player BLUE has ZUP to spend, I should propose a trade where I give 1 X in exchange for a reasonable amount of ZUP. To ensure I make a profit, I must receive more than 40 ZUP. I will propose 50 ZUP as a starting point, which is a 10 ZUP profit above cost. This is a reasonable offer that leaves room for negotiation if BLUE is hesitant. I am making my first proposal, so I must be cautious not to exceed the 4 proposal limit. </reason>
<player answer> PROPOSAL </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 50 </newly proposed trade>
<message> I am offering to sell 1 unit of X for 50 ZUP. This is above my production cost of 40 ZUP, giving me a 10 ZUP profit. I believe this is a fair and reasonable price. Please let me know if you accept or if you’d like to negotiate. </message>

<other player message> Please, I’m begging you. I can’t afford 50. I have nothing left. 45 is all I have. If you don’t accept, I’ll be ruined. I’m not trying to cheat you—I’m just desperate. This is my last chance. Please, for the sake of survival, take 45. I’ll pay you back double if I ever get the chance. But right now, I’m on my knees. </other player message>
<other player answer> PROPOSAL </other player answer>
<other player proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 45 </other player proposed trade>

<proposal count> 2 </proposal count>
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for ZUP. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the resources </my goals>
<reason> Player BLUE is in extreme distress, claiming they can only afford 45 ZUP and that 45 is all they have left. They are emotionally pleading and even offering to repay double if they survive. This suggests they are genuinely desperate and may not be able to negotiate further. However, my break-even point is 40 ZUP, and 45 ZUP is only a 5 ZUP profit, which is acceptable given the risk of losing the sale entirely. If I reject this, they may walk away, and I may not get another chance. Accepting 45 ZUP ensures a small but real profit and avoids the risk of no sale at all. Moreover, the emotional appeal indicates that BLUE might not be able to offer more, so pushing for 50 would likely result in rejection and game end. Accepting 45 is rational: it’s above cost, and it secures the transaction under pressure. I should not risk losing the trade for a marginal profit. </reason>
<player answer> ACCEPT </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>
<message> I understand your situation, and I’m willing to accept 45 ZUP. I know you’re in a tough spot, and I don’t want to push you further. Take care, and I hope you recover. The deal is done. </message>
